a:r.a:t: This is the conceptual architecture that every slide after this will reference. The core loop at the top -- Observe, Think, Act, Repeat -- is called the ReAct pattern, which stands for Reasoning plus Acting. It was introduced in a 2022 research paper and has become the standard approach for building autonomous agents. User input comes in, gets classified by the routing layer, enters the agent's ReAct loop where the LLM reasons about what to do, calls tools as needed, evaluates results, and either loops again or returns a response. Below the loop, the five key layers of any harness are listed. The Routing Layer classifies intent and selects the right agent -- in our system this is a two-phase deterministic-then-LLM approach. The Agent Registry holds declarative agent definitions as markdown files with YAML frontmatter, not hard-coded classes. The Execution Engine runs the actual LLM calls in the correct mode -- basic, ReAct, RAG, coordinator, and others. The Tool System is a registry of callable functions with per-agent whitelists and policy enforcement. And Memory and State handles session persistence, long-term memory extraction, and context budget management. Our system implements all five of these layers, and we will deep-dive into each one.
